faq.question: What time is it in Vienna right now?
faq.answer: Vienna currently observes Central European Time (CET, UTC+1) during winter months and Central European Summer Time (CEST, UTC+2) during daylight saving time. The city follows the same timezone as most of continental Europe, making it 6 hours ahead of New York during winter and 5 hours behind Tokyo year-round.

faq.question: What time zone is Vienna in?
faq.answer: Vienna operates on Central European Time (CET, UTC+1) from late October to late March, and Central European Summer Time (CEST, UTC+2) from late March to late October. This timezone is shared with major European capitals including Berlin, Rome, and Prague, facilitating seamless business coordination across the EU.

faq.question: Does Vienna observe daylight saving time?
faq.answer: Yes. Vienna observes daylight saving time, switching to Central European Summer Time (CEST, UTC+2) on the last Sunday of March at 02:00 CET, and reverting to Central European Time (CET, UTC+1) on the last Sunday of October at 03:00 CEST. This practice aligns with EU Directive 2000/84/EC, though future changes may occur following ongoing EU discussions about abolishing seasonal time changes.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Vienna and New York?
faq.answer: Vienna is 6 hours ahead of New York during winter (when Vienna uses CET and New York uses EST), and 6 hours ahead during summer (CEST vs EDT). When it's 12:00 noon in Vienna, it's 06:00 AM in New York. The time difference remains constant year-round because both cities change clocks on different dates but maintain the same offset relationship.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Vienna and London?
faq.answer: Vienna is 1 hour ahead of London throughout the year. When it's 15:00 (3:00 PM) in Vienna, it's 14:00 (2:00 PM) in London. Both cities observe daylight saving time with synchronized transitions on the last Sunday of March and October, maintaining the consistent 1-hour difference that simplifies business coordination between Austrian and British markets.

faq.question: What time does the stock exchange open in Vienna?
faq.answer: The Vienna Stock Exchange (Wiener Börse) opens at 09:00 CET/CEST and closes at 17:30 CET/CEST on weekdays. Pre-trading begins at 08:00 and post-trading extends until 17:45. This schedule overlaps with Frankfurt (identical hours), London (08:00-16:30 GMT), and catches the first 2.5 hours of New York trading (09:30-16:00 EST), making Vienna strategically positioned for European equity trading.

faq.question: What is the best time to call Vienna from the US?
faq.answer: The optimal window to call Vienna from the US East Coast is 03:00-11:00 EST (09:00-17:00 CET in Vienna), catching standard Austrian business hours. From the West Coast, call between 00:00-08:00 PST. For personal calls respecting evening hours, US East Coast callers should reach out before 13:00 EST to catch Vienna before 19:00 local time.

faq.question: What are typical business hours in Vienna?
faq.answer: Standard business hours in Vienna run Monday-Friday 09:00-17:00 CET/CEST, with many offices closing for lunch 12:00-13:00. Austrian business culture emphasizes punctuality, with meetings typically scheduled between 09:00-12:00 or 14:00-16:00. Banks operate 08:00-15:00 weekdays, while retail shops generally open 09:00-18:30 Monday-Friday and 09:00-18:00 Saturday, though many close earlier on Saturdays.

seo_description: Current time in Vienna, Austria. UTC+1/+2 timezone, CET/CEST info, DST dates, time differences with NYC, London & global cities. Updated live.

content_blocks.title: Vienna Time Zone Guide
content_blocks.content: Vienna operates on Central European Time (CET, UTC+1) during standard time and Central European Summer Time (CEST, UTC+2) during daylight saving periods. The Austrian capital shares its timezone with 35+ European countries, creating a unified business corridor from Spain to Poland. Clock changes occur on the last Sunday of March (02:00→03:00) and last Sunday of October (03:00→02:00), synchronized across the EU per Directive 2000/84/EC. This positioning makes Vienna exactly 6 hours ahead of New York year-round, 1 hour ahead of London, and 7 hours behind Singapore. The Vienna International Centre and numerous multinational headquarters leverage this strategic temporal position for coordinating operations across three continents during the 09:00-17:00 CET working window. Compare Vienna and New York time difference or check Vienna vs Berlin timezone for neighboring coordination.

content_blocks.title: Business Hours in Vienna
content_blocks.content: Vienna's business culture operates on a disciplined 09:00-17:00 CET/CEST schedule Monday through Friday, with many organizations observing a traditional 12:00-13:00 lunch break—a practice less common in other European capitals but maintained in Austrian corporate culture. The Vienna Stock Exchange (Wiener Börse) opens precisely at 09:00 and closes at 17:30, with extended trading sessions bookending the core hours (08:00 pre-trading, 17:45 post-trading closure). This schedule creates a 4-hour overlap with London's LSE (09:00-17:30 CET = 08:00-16:30 GMT) and captures the critical 15:30-17:30 CET window when both European and US markets operate simultaneously. Financial institutions including Bank Austria and Erste Group maintain stricter 08:00-15:00 customer-facing hours. For international coordination, Vienna's business day intersects with New York from 15:30-17:00 CET (09:30-11:00 EST), making late afternoon the premium window for transatlantic video conferences. Use our Meeting Time Planner to schedule across Vienna, New York, and London simultaneously.

content_blocks.title: Calling Vienna Internationally
content_blocks.content: Reaching Vienna from the United States requires strategic timing to respect Austrian cultural norms around work-life boundaries. From the US East Coast (EST/EDT), the optimal calling window spans 03:00-11:00 EST, corresponding to Vienna's 09:00-17:00 business hours. West Coast callers (PST/PDT) face a more challenging 00:00-08:00 PST window, making early morning calls necessary for same-day business. For personal calls, Austrians typically dine 18:00-20:00 CET, so US East Coast callers should avoid reaching out after 13:00 EST (19:00 Vienna time) unless previously arranged. The 6-hour constant offset simplifies mental math: add 6 hours to EST or 9 hours to PST year-round. UK callers enjoy the most convenient access with just a 1-hour difference—London's 09:00 GMT equals Vienna's 10:00 CET. Asian business contacts from Singapore (+7 hours ahead of Vienna) should call 02:00-10:00 SGT to reach Vienna office hours. The Vienna-London time converter and Vienna-Singapore comparison tools eliminate calculation errors for recurring international coordination.

timezone_facts.dst_start: Last Sunday of March at 02:00 CET (clocks forward to 03:00 CEST)
timezone_facts.dst_end: Last Sunday of October at 03:00 CEST (clocks back to 02:00 CET)
